stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {s : String}, Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s → Γ = [Tp.atom s] ∨ (∃ L B A Δ Λ, Γ = L ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ ∧ Δ ⇒ A ∧ L ++ [B] ++ Λ ⇒ Tp.atom s) ∨ (∃ Γ₁ Δ A B R, Γ = Γ₁ ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ R ∧ Δ ⇒ A ∧ Γ₁ ++ [B] ++ R ⇒ Tp.atom s)
∀ {A : Tp} {s : String}, [A] ⇒ Tp.atom s ↔ A = Tp.atom s
Every derivation of Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s admits a finite focused decomposition tree obtained by iterating the atomic-succedent last-step analysis until every leaf is a singleton atomic axiom.

For atomic goals, successful runs of `proveAux` are in height-preserving correspondence with focused decomposition trees: `proveAux n Γ (Tp.atom s) = true` exactly when there exists a focused atomic decomposition tree for `Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s` of height at most `n`.
∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, Γ ⇒ A ↔ ∃ n : Nat, FocusedTree n Γ A
∀ {n : Nat} {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, proveAux n Γ A = true ↔ FocusedTree n Γ A
∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {m : Nat}, list_degree Γ + tp_degree A + 1 ≤ m → (Γ ⇒ A ↔ FocusedTree m Γ A)

There exist Γ and A such that every member of Γ is atomic, Γ ⇒ A is derivable in the full product-free calculus, but the corresponding sequent is not derivable in either the left-only fragment or the right-only fragment.

For every context `Γ`, the list `candidates Γ` is an exact classification of the possible last principal steps of derivations with atomic succedent: a derivation of `Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s` realizes one of those candidates exactly when the two recursive subgoals attached to that candidate are derivable.
∀ Γ A, AtomicCtx Γ → ((LeftRep Γ A ∨ RightRep Γ A) ↔ (Γ ⇒ A ∧ every division connective occurring in A has the same orientation))

Characterize exactly those sequents `Γ ⇒ A` for which every formula in `Γ` together with `A` has one common division orientation, and prove that on precisely this class the full calculus and the corresponding one-sided fragment have the same derivable sequents.
Prove that a derivable sequent `Γ ⇒ A` is one-sided representable if and only if it admits a cut-free derivation in which every division-rule occurrence has the same orientation.

For every sequent Γ ⇒ A, there exists a cut-free derivation of Γ ⇒ A in which every division-rule occurrence is left-oriented or every division-rule occurrence is right-oriented if and only if LeftRep Γ A ∨ RightRep Γ A.

For every derivable sequent Γ ⇒ A, if every formula in A :: Γ is LeftDivisionOnly then Γ ⇒ A is derivable already in the pure left fragment, and if every formula in A :: Γ is RightDivisionOnly then Γ ⇒ A is derivable already in the pure right fragment.
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (((∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, LeftDivisionOnly x) ∧ Γ ⇒ A) → HasLeftOrientedDerivation Γ A) ∧ (((∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, RightDivisionOnly x) ∧ Γ ⇒ A) → HasRightOrientedDerivation Γ A)
For every context Γ and type A, LeftRep Γ A ∧ RightRep Γ A if and only if Γ ⇒ A and every formula in A :: Γ is atomic.
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), HasLeftOrientedDerivation Γ A ↔ ((∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, LeftDivisionOnly x) ∧ Γ ⇒ A)
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), HasRightOrientedDerivation Γ A ↔ ((∀ x ∈ A :: Γ, RightDivisionOnly x) ∧ Γ ⇒ A)
For all Γ, Δ, Λ, A, B, if LeftRep Γ A and LeftRep (Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ) B then LeftRep (Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ) B, and if RightRep Γ A and RightRep (Δ ++ [A] ++ Λ) B then RightRep (Δ ++ Γ ++ Λ) B.

For every context Γ and type A, if every formula in A :: Γ is LeftDivisionOnly and Γ ⇒ A, then there exists a focused proof tree for Γ ⇒ A such that every node Δ ⇒ B in the tree also satisfies ∀ x ∈ B :: Δ, LeftDivisionOnly x.
For every nonempty context Γ and types A, B, LeftRep Γ (A ⧹ B) if and only if LeftRep (A :: Γ) B.

For every context Γ and type A, if every formula in A :: Γ is RightDivisionOnly and Γ ⇒ A, then there exists a focused proof tree for Γ ⇒ A such that every node Δ ⇒ B in the tree also satisfies ∀ x ∈ B :: Δ, RightDivisionOnly x.
For every nonempty context Γ and types A, B, RightRep Γ (B ⧸ A) if and only if RightRep (Γ ++ [A]) B.
`∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (∃ n : Nat, LeftFocusedTree n Γ A) ↔ ((LeftOnlyNode Γ A) ∧ Γ ⇒ A)`
∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (∃ n : Nat, RightFocusedTree n Γ A) ↔ ((RightOnlyNode Γ A) ∧ Γ ⇒ A)

For every context Γ and atom s, LeftRep Γ (Tp.atom s) iff either Γ = [Tp.atom s] or there exist contexts Π, Δ, Λ and types A, B such that Γ = Π ++ Δ ++ [A ⧹ B] ++ Λ, LeftRep Δ A, and LeftRep (Π ++ [B] ++ Λ) (Tp.atom s).

For every context Γ and atom s, RightRep Γ (Tp.atom s) iff either Γ = [Tp.atom s] or there exist contexts Π, Δ, Λ and types A, B such that Γ = Π ++ [B ⧸ A] ++ Δ ++ Λ, RightRep Δ A, and RightRep (Π ++ [B] ++ Λ) (Tp.atom s).

For every context Γ and type A, RightRep Γ A iff there exist a context ΓR and a type AR in the pure right fragment such that Γ and A are exactly the translations of ΓR and AR into the full product-free language and ΓR ⇒ AR in the pure right calculus.

∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), LeftRep Γ A ↔ ∃ ΓL : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp, ∃ AL : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp, Γ = ΓL.map Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.toProductFree ∧ A = AL.toProductFree ∧ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent ΓL AL
